items.title: The National | 2 teens accused of planning joint school attacks
items.summary_auto: March 18, 2026 | A Nova Scotia teen and a Manitoba teen are accused of planning co-ordinated violent attacks on their local schools. Ontario Premier Doug Ford praises a homeowner for shooting an intruder. And Rideau Cottage is deemed 'inadequate' as a home for the Prime Minister.

items.title: Pierre Poilievre sits down for interview with podcaster Joe Rogan
items.summary_auto: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre is set to appear on The Joe Rogan Experience, one of the world's most popular podcasts.

items.title: Ont. woman pleads guilty to manslaughter in mother's 2010 death after new trial ordered
items.summary_auto: Jennifer Pan has pleaded guilty to manslaughter in the 2010 death of her mother, nearly a year after the Supreme Court of Canada ordered a new first-degree murder trial for the Markham, Ont., woman in a case that drew international attention and spawned a Netflix documentary.

items.title: Jennifer Pan pleads guilty to manslaughter in mother's death after new trial ordered
items.summary_auto: Jennifer Pan has pleaded guilty to manslaughter in the 2010 death of her mother, nearly a year after the Supreme Court of Canada ordered a new first-degree murder trial for the Markham, Ont., woman in a case that drew international attention and spawned a Netflix documentary.

items.title: Cuba's president lashes out at Trump's threats to take the island as regional support weakens
items.summary_auto: Cuban President Miguel Diaz-Canel has lashed out after U.S. President Donald Trump said that he can do "whatever he wants" with the Caribbean island and that Washington could take "imminent action" against it.

items.title: Last detainee from Trump’s 2025 Gaza war protest crackdown freed after a year
items.summary_auto: For Leqaa Kordia, her newfound freedom means little to her until “everybody is free." “There is a lot of injustice in this place,” Kordia told reporters after emerging from a U.S. immigration detention centre in Texas on Monday. “There are a lot of people that shouldn’t be here in the first place. We’re going to keep fighting for them.”

items.title: P.E.I. health minister remembered for his leadership and dedication to community, Islanders
items.summary_auto: Mark McLane is being remembered for his humour and his leadership. The health minister and Progressive Conservative MLA for Cornwall-Meadowbank died Tuesday at 56 after a serious illness.

items.title: Accused extortionists deported as part of CBSA crackdown on cross-Canada network
items.summary_auto: Following extensive CBC News reporting on two Indian nationals deported for alleged links to the extortion crisis plaguing Canadian communities, the Canada Border Services Agency has identified the two men as proof of expanded efforts to disrupt extortion networks.

items.title: Toronto Tempo president ‘excited’ for team’s first tip-off after WNBA, players reach agreement in principle
items.summary_auto: In the wee hours of Wednesday morning, Toronto Tempo president Teresa Resch heard her phone ring from another room. Sure enough, in a group chat with the WNBA’s other team presidents, news was spreading that there was indeed a deal in principle on a collective bargaining agreement. Now, things are about to get real.

items.title: Ont. premier, officials call for reversal of temporary release of convicted child killer
items.summary_auto: A recent temporary release of a child murderer in Durham Region is drawing criticism from top officials across Ontario, with calls for the parole board to keep him behind bars before he’s let out again.

items.title: Canadians in Middle East warned not to record or share war videos
items.summary_auto: Global Affairs Canada is warning Canadians in the Middle East to refrain from documenting military activity as war engulfs the region. In a post on X Tuesday, the government warned that "taking, sharing or possessing" photos or videos of military activity or damage "could lead to fines, imprisonment or deportation." The post specifically mentions Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabi and the United Arab Emirates.

items.title: Alberta bill would limit medically assisted dying to patients facing 'reasonably foreseeable' death
items.summary_auto: New legislation would prevent anyone in Alberta from receiving medical assistance in dying (MAID) who is unlikely to die within the next 12 months.

items.title: Canadian Olympic bronze-medal ice dancers Gilles, Poirier head to world championships
items.summary_auto: Five weeks after earning their first Olympic medal, Canadian ice dancers Piper Gilles and Paul Poirier are gearing up for their 13th world championships, next week in Prague. "We want to enjoy ourselves, enjoy our skating [and] celebrate what we've accomplished this season," Gilles says.

items.title: Ontario man accused in mob-related killings arrested in Mexico after 9 years on the run
items.summary_auto: A Hamilton man who was on the run the past nine years was arrested in Mexico in connection with two mob-related killings and faces at least two murder counts. Daniel Tomassetti, 35, was arrested in August 2025, police told CBC Hamilton, and efforts “are underway to facilitate his extradition to Canada."

items.title: Sask. budget projects $819M deficit, no return to surplus until 2030
items.summary_auto: Titled "Protecting Saskatchewan," the budget echoes a refrain that Premier Scott Moe and Reiter have repeated in the weeks before the budget — no tax increases or service cuts.

items.title: No big-ticket items, lower deficit in Quebec's modest pre-election budget
items.summary_auto: With the looming October election, Quebec Finance Minister Eric Girard has delivered a modest budget as the province aims to withstand ongoing trade disruptions and mounting geopolitical pressure.

items.title: Montreal airport fully operational after suspicious package temporarily closes runway
items.summary_auto: A suspicious package temporarily reduced air traffic at Montreal's Pierre Elliott Trudeau International Airport on Wednesday.

items.title: Boy dead after being struck by train in Mississauga, Ont.: police
items.summary_auto: A boy was fatally struck by a westbound GO train in Mississauga on Wednesday afternoon, police say.

items.title: Boy dead after being struck by GO train in Mississauga, police say
items.summary_auto: A boy was fatally struck by a westbound GO train in Mississauga on Wednesday afternoon, police say.

items.title: Police chief stands by decision to deploy sniper at St. Patrick's Day street party in Waterloo, Ont.
items.summary_auto: The Waterloo Regional Police Service's chief is standing by the decision to deploy a tactical officer to an unsanctioned St. Patrick's Day street party in Waterloo, Ont., after sightings of what appeared to be a sniper were posted on social media.

items.title: Canada's population shrank last year — a first for the country, StatsCan says
items.summary_auto: Canada's population dropped last year, marking the first time the country has seen an annual net decline in residents since Confederation.

items.title: U.S. civil rights leader Dolores Huerta says Cesar Chavez sexually assaulted her in the 1960s
items.summary_auto: Civil rights ​leader Dolores Huerta, who co-founded the United Farm Workers union with Cesar Chavez and fought alongside him for decades to expand labour rights in the U.S., on Wednesday accused Chavez of sexually assaulting her in the 1960s.

items.title: While Canada pumps the brakes on robotaxis, China hits the gas
items.summary_auto: Canada is still figuring out regulations when it comes to driverless vehicles, like robotaxis, on its roads. China, on the other hand, has embraced the technology, rolling out fully autonomous vehicles in select areas of cities like Beijing, Wuhan and Shanghai. CBC’s Lisa Xing and Chris Brown are in China as part of special coverage and took a ride inside a vehicle to see what it’s like.

items.title: An AI Val Kilmer is set to posthumously appear in a new movie
items.summary_auto: A year after the actor's death, a generative AI version of Val Kilmer will co-star in an independent film, in one of the boldest uses yet of artificial intelligence in moviemaking.

items.title: EV sales targets in Quebec and B.C. are hurting Canada's auto market, Ont. premier says
items.summary_auto: Ontario Premier Doug Ford says electric vehicle sales targets in British Columbia and Quebec are making Canada less competitive. He asked his fellow premiers to scale back their mandates as the country feels the effects of Trump's tariffs.

items.title: 'Should have shot him a couple more times': Ford congratulates person who shot alleged Ontario home invader
items.summary_auto: Ontario Premier Doug Ford congratulated a homeowner who shot and injured an alleged home invader in Vaughan, Ont., this week, saying intruders "need to be shot."

items.title: Pakistan and Afghanistan announce temporary pause in fighting
items.summary_auto: Pakistan and Afghanistan have declared a temporary pause in escalating fighting, two days after Kabul blamed Islamabad for a deadly airstrike in the Afghan capital that it said killed hundreds of people at a drug rehabilitation hospital.

items.title: N.S., Manitoba teens accused of planning co-ordinated school attacks
items.summary_auto: Police say two teens — a girl from the town of Bridgewater, N.S., and the other from Manitoba — have been charged for allegedly planning simultaneous attacks at their local schools.

items.title: Trump has delayed the Beijing summit. China wonders if he'll ever come to the negotiating table
items.summary_auto: The question echoing through Beijing’s diplomatic and political circles is whether U.S. President Donald Trump will show up for a summit in Beijing that was originally supposed to take place this month. Distracted by a self-launched war in Iran and facing mounting domestic pressure, Trump has pushed the China file to the side to focus on war strategy.

items.title: Evacuation order issued for Central Coast hamlet as heavy rain batters parts of B.C.
items.summary_auto: Part of the hamlet of Martin Valley, located northeast of Bella Bella, is under an evacuation order as of Tuesday due to "landslide and flood hazard risk."

items.title: Bank of Canada holds key interest rate at 2.25%, saying war will boost global inflation
items.summary_auto: The Bank of Canada held its key interest rate at 2.25 per cent on Wednesday, saying that higher oil and gas prices from the war in the Middle East are likely to boost global inflation but that it's too soon to assess the conflict's impact on the Canadian economy.

items.title: Child vaccination rate drops sharply in Michigan as RFK Jr. influences policy
items.summary_auto: Reuters data analysis shows swift fall in inoculations of toddlers. Officials link vaccination ​decline to Kennedy's policies and rhetoric.

items.title: Iran hits Qatar energy hub after its Pars oil field struck
items.summary_auto: Iran accused Israel of striking its facilities in the huge Pars gas field on Wednesday in a major escalation in the U.S.-Israeli war that sent oil prices ‌shooting higher, and retaliated ‌by vowing attacks on oil and gas targets throughout the Gulf, firing missiles at Qatar and Saudi Arabia.

items.title: Israeli airstrikes in Beirut intensify, demolishing a 10-storey building and striking bridges
items.summary_auto: Israel stepped up airstrikes on Beirut on Wednesday, killing at least 10 people and destroying a 10-storey building near the city center in the third week of its war with Hezbollah, Lebanese authorities said.

items.title: As gas prices rise, ride-hail drivers feel especially pinched at the pump
items.summary_auto: Gas prices worldwide have soared since the U.S. and Israel launched strikes on Iran, and the widening war has pinched tanker traffic in the crucially important Strait of Hormuz shipping route. Ride-hail drivers, many of whom are on the road for hours a day, say they're being hit hard by the extra expense.

items.title: Diesel prices surge to highest since 2022, raising concern for shipping, consumer costs
items.summary_auto: The ongoing U.S.-Iran war is causing diesel prices to spike across the country, climbing nearly 30 per cent since the beginning of March. In some parts of Quebec, a litre of diesel is selling for almost $2.50.

items.title: Science has an Epstein problem. Women in paleontology say it's a symptom of a deeper misogyny
items.summary_auto: The names of hundreds of scientists have appeared in the Epstein files, including prominent dinosaur researchers. As the paleontology community reckons with the fallout of these revelations, women in the field say it's bringing to the forefront what they've known for years — that paleontology is rampant with abuse and harassment, funding and fame are prioritized above all else and institutions protect established men at the top at the expense of younger women trying to build careers.

items.title: Self-harm among young Canadians is on the rise, specifically in girls, new research finds
items.summary_auto: A growing number of young people are turning to self-harm in Canada and other parts of the world, according to new research.
